data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality over quantity, focusing on movement efficiency and maintaining good form throughout. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of a mix of cardio, strength, and bodyweight movements. The goal here is to keep a steady pace, focus on your technique, and really feel each movement. Let’s break it down!


Workout Components


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • For the Row: Sit on the rower with your feet secured. Grab the handle, and start with your knees bent and body leaning slightly forward.
  • Push through your legs first, then lean back slightly as you pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close.
  • Reverse the motion: extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the start.
  • For the Ski Erg: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grasp the handles with both hands.
  • Initiate the movement by pulling down with your arms while simultaneously bending your knees slightly, then return to the starting position.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and core engaged.
  • In the row, avoid overextending your back; keep the motion fluid.
  • In the Ski Erg, focus on using your arms and core rather than just your legs.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If you’re new to rowing, aim for a slower pace to focus on form.
  • For the Ski Erg, reduce the time to 2:00 if needed.

    • 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand in front of the box with your feet hip-width apart.
  • Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box.
  • Bring your other foot up to meet the first, then step back down one foot at a time.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your chest up and core tight.
  • Ensure your entire foot is on the box when stepping up.
  • Alternate legs for each rep to balance the workload.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height.
  • If you need more challenge, hold a dumbbell in each hand.

    • 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out. Place the kettlebell between your feet.
  • Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to grab the kettlebell with both hands.
  • Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up along your body to your chin, elbows high.
  • Lower the kettlebell back down to the ground and repeat.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and chest up during the lift.
  • Use your legs to drive the movement, not just your arms.
  • Ensure your elbows are above your wrists at the top of the pull.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lighter kettlebell or perform the movement with just bodyweight to focus on form.
  • If kettlebells are unavailable, you can substitute with dumbbells.

    • 4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Set the rings at a height where you can comfortably reach them while standing.
  • Grab the rings and lean back, keeping your body straight from head to heels.
  • Pull your chest towards the rings, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower back down.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your body straight; avoid sagging your hips or arching your back.
  • Engage your core throughout the movement.
  • The closer your feet are to the rings, the harder the movement will be.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a bar or use a TRX.
  • You can also perform bodyweight rows on a low bar or table.

    • Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 2:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 16/8
  • Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 12kg/6kg
  • Ring Rows: 8 reps

  • **Train:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 20/12
  • Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 16kg/8kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

  • **Compete:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 24/16
  • Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 24kg/16kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

    • Strategy Advice

  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to go slower and maintain good form than to rush and risk injury.
  • Break up the movements into manageable sets if you start to feel fatigued, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows.
  • Remember to breathe and stay engaged with your core to support your movements.

    • Alright team, let’s get after it! Keep your form sharp, and let’s make this a great workout!

combined_content.title: CrossFit MINS Workout

WOD:

For Quality

6 Rounds:

  • 3:00 Row/Ski Erg
  • 20 Box Step-ups
  • 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
  • 10 Ring Rows

    • Sweat: 16/8

    Train: 20/12

    Compete: 24/16

Let’s break it down! ## Workout Components ### 1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00) - **Technique Steps:** - For the Row: Sit on the rower with your feet secured. Grab the handle, and start with your knees bent and body leaning slightly forward. - Push through your legs first, then lean back slightly as you pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close. - Reverse the motion: extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the start. - For the Ski Erg: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grasp the handles with both hands. - Initiate the movement by pulling down with your arms while simultaneously bending your knees slightly, then return to the starting position. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and core engaged. - In the row, avoid overextending your back; keep the motion fluid. - In the Ski Erg, focus on using your arms and core rather than just your legs. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If you’re new to rowing, aim for a slower pace to focus on form. - For the Ski Erg, reduce the time to 2:00 if needed. ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand in front of the box with your feet hip-width apart. - Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box. - Bring your other foot up to meet the first, then step back down one foot at a time. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your chest up and core tight. - Ensure your entire foot is on the box when stepping up. - Alternate legs for each rep to balance the workload. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height. - If you need more challenge, hold a dumbbell in each hand. ### 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out. Place the kettlebell between your feet. - Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to grab the kettlebell with both hands. - Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up along your body to your chin, elbows high. - Lower the kettlebell back down to the ground and repeat. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and chest up during the lift. - Use your legs to drive the movement, not just your arms. - Ensure your elbows are above your wrists at the top of the pull. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lighter kettlebell or perform the movement with just bodyweight to focus on form. - If kettlebells are unavailable, you can substitute with dumbbells. ### 4. Ring Rows (10 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Set the rings at a height where you can comfortably reach them while standing. - Grab the rings and lean back, keeping your body straight from head to heels. - Pull your chest towards the rings, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower back down. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your body straight; avoid sagging your hips or arching your back. - Engage your core throughout the movement. - The closer your feet are to the rings, the harder the movement will be. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a bar or use a TRX. - You can also perform bodyweight rows on a low bar or table. ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat:** - Row/Ski Erg: 2:00 - Box Step-Ups: 16/8 - Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 12kg/6kg - Ring Rows: 8 reps - **Train:** - Row/Ski Erg: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 20/12 - Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 16kg/8kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps - **Compete:** - Row/Ski Erg: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 24/16 - Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls: 24kg/16kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps ### Strategy Advice - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to go slower and maintain good form than to rush and risk injury. - Break up the movements into manageable sets if you start to feel fatigued, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows. - Remember to breathe and stay engaged with your core to support your movements. Alright team, let’s get after it! Keep your form sharp, and let’s make this a great workout!
